verses.text: Jeremiah writes a letter to the elders carried captive to Babylon. He shepherds them from afar. God's care follows His people into exile.
Jeremiah writes a letter to the elders carried captive to Babylon. He shepherds them from afar. God's care follows His people into exile.
Jeremiah writes a letter to the elders carried captive to Babylon. He shepherds them from afar. God's care follows His people into exile.

He bids them build houses, plant gardens, marry, and 'seek the peace of the city... for in the peace thereof shall ye have peace.' Settle and pray for it. God's people are to seek the good of the place where He has set them.

He bids them build houses, plant gardens, marry, and 'seek the peace of the city... for in the peace thereof shall ye have peace.' Settle and pray for it. God's people are to seek the good of the place where He has set them.

He bids them build houses, plant gardens, marry, and 'seek the peace of the city... for in the peace thereof shall ye have peace.' Settle and pray for it. God's people are to seek the good of the place where He has set them.

He bids them build houses, plant gardens, marry, and 'seek the peace of the city... for in the peace thereof shall ye have peace.' Settle and pray for it. God's people are to seek the good of the place where He has set them.
Let no prophets among them deceive them, for 'they prophesy falsely... in my name.' False hope abounds even in exile. The captives must beware flattering lies as much as those at home.
Let no prophets among them deceive them, for 'they prophesy falsely... in my name.' False hope abounds even in exile. The captives must beware flattering lies as much as those at home.

After seventy years God will visit them, 'for I know the thoughts that I think toward you... thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.' His plans are good. Even in chastening, God purposes a hopeful future for His own.

After seventy years God will visit them, 'for I know the thoughts that I think toward you... thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you an expected end.' His plans are good. Even in chastening, God purposes a hopeful future for His own.

'Then shall ye... seek me, and find me, when ye shall search for me with all your heart,' and He will end their captivity. Wholehearted seeking finds God. He is found of those who seek Him in earnest.

'Then shall ye... seek me, and find me, when ye shall search for me with all your heart,' and He will end their captivity. Wholehearted seeking finds God. He is found of those who seek Him in earnest.

'Then shall ye... seek me, and find me, when ye shall search for me with all your heart,' and He will end their captivity. Wholehearted seeking finds God. He is found of those who seek Him in earnest.
But those left in Jerusalem, refusing to hear, shall meet sword, famine, and plague, like bad figs. Their refusal dooms them. The unrepentant at home fare worse than the chastened in exile.

But those left in Jerusalem, refusing to hear, shall meet sword, famine, and plague, like bad figs. Their refusal dooms them. The unrepentant at home fare worse than the chastened in exile.
Against the lying prophets Ahab and Zedekiah, who committed adultery and lies, Babylon shall roast them in the fire. Their sin is repaid. False prophets who corrupt others meet a terrible end.

Shemaiah wrote letters urging the priests to silence Jeremiah as a madman. He opposed God's word. Persecutors still try to muzzle the faithful messenger.

Shemaiah wrote letters urging the priests to silence Jeremiah as a madman. He opposed God's word. Persecutors still try to muzzle the faithful messenger.
God declares Shemaiah shall be punished and 'not behold the good that I will do for my people.' He forfeits the blessing. Those who fight God's word lose their share in its promises.
